title: Bungo Bung
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Bungo}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Bungo-AJ11-full.png]] |name=Bungo Bung |homeworld=[[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]]{{JournalCite|11|Bungo n' Rusti Get Carry-Out}} |birth= |death= |species=[[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents=[[Bungo Bung's father]] |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|My "master" was enquiring as to whether you plan on taking all the Bantha Breakfast Biscuits.''"
"''You tell Mouseman that we will take what we want! And he'll be lucky if we don't shove what's left down his throat!|42-RST and Captain Wankle after the Imperial captain confiscated a shipment of Bungo Bung's Bantha Breakfast Biscuits|}} '''Bungo Bung''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]]. Despite growing up working on his [[Bungo Bung's father|father's]] [[boaboo fungus]] [[Farm/Legends|farm]], Bung harbored a desire to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. Joining [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation's]] [[SoroSuub Transport and Supply Division|Transport and Supply Division]], he eventually attained the [[Commander/Legends|command]] of the ''[[Chubby Gundark]]'', a [[Nyubba-class cargo barge driver|''Nyubba''-class cargo barge driver]]. Assisted by his [[Droid/Legends|droid]] co-pilot, [[42-RST]]—known as Rusti—Bung was the pioneer of the [[Great Bantha Breakfast Biscuit Run]] between Sullust and [[Gastrula]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Bung transported [[Biscuit Baron]]'s [[Bantha Breakfast Biscuit]]s to the native [[Gastrulan]]s, despite interference on the first run from [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Wankle]]. Although he made a healthy profit, Bung broke even after spending much of the [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]] on repairs to his ship after an encounter with a [[Giant Amorphous Bantha Breakfast Biscuit]], a strange anomalous lifeform that was the result of the interaction between the preservatives in Bantha Breakfast Biscuits and the stale [[Atmosphere/Legends|air]] in his ship. He survived by defeating the creature with [[blue sauce]], which, as the recommended accompaniment to a Bantha Breakfast Biscuit, proved to be the lifeform's weakness. ==Biography== ===Early life and employment with SoroSuub Corporation=== {{Quote|We can't do that! 'It's against company policy to …'"
"'… to carry non-SoroSuub cargo.' I know.|Captain Bungo Bung and 42-RST|}} [[File:Chubbygundark.jpg|thumb|left|The ''Chubby Gundark'']] The [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Bungo Bung was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]], and grew up in the underground caverns of his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], working on his [[Bungo Bung's father|father's]] [[boaboo fungus]] [[Farm/Legends|farm]]. Bung had few duties and spent much of his [[Time/Legends|time]] sitting around and daydreaming. The [[Fungus/Legends|fungus]] was tended to by [[Droid/Legends|droids]], who then crushed the fungus to make [[boaboo juice]]. He was only required to clean up the mess the droids made when they eventually short-circuited in the moist environment, and transport the juice to the markets in [[G'rinn Go city]]. However, Bung's desire was to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] a [[Starship/Legends|starship]], and when he was old enough, the young Sullustan signed up with the [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation's]] [[SoroSuub Transport and Supply Division|Transport and Supply Division]]. Serving as a shipping pilot, Bung faithfully worked for SoroSuub for a number of [[Standard year/Legends|years]], eventually earning the [[Captain/Legends|captaincy]] of the [[Nyubba-class cargo barge driver|''Nyubba''-class cargo barge driver]] ''[[Chubby Gundark]]''. During his travels, Bung worked for the [[Mephout]], and they gave him a gift—the [[RST Model Translator Droid]] [[42-RST]], otherwise known as "Rusti." Although Bung believed that the gift was for the services he had rendered for them, the Mephout were simply getting rid of obsolete technology. 42-RST served as co-pilot of the ''Chubby Gundark'' and was employed as a translator. ===The Great Bantha Breakfast Biscuit Run=== {{Quote|Do you know how many people in the Empire have never tasted one of these?''"
"''How many?''"
"''Lots!''"
"''Really? Then why not load up the ship with Bantha Breakfast Biscuits and spread the word around the galaxy?|Captain Bungo Bung and 42-RST|}} [[File:Rusti.jpg|thumb|right|230px|Bung's droid co-pilot, 42-RST, otherwise known as "Rusti"]] Sometime during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Captain Bung and 42-RST returned to Sullust after a [[Transport/Legends|transport]] run. Bung, hungry as ever, stopped at his favorite [[Restaurant/Legends|eating establishment]], [[Biscuit Baron]]. During the meal, he attempted to explain his love of [[Bantha Breakfast Biscuit]]s to 42-RST, although she could not comprehend. Bung made an off-hand comment about how a lot of people in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] had never tasted a Breakfast Biscuit, and 42-RST came up with the idea of loading the ''Chubby Gundark'' with the [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] item and finding a buyer for them. Initially balking at the idea since it was against SoroSuub regulations to transport non-SoroSuub goods, Bung relented and spent a [[Standard month/Legends|month's]] wages on forty cases of Breakfast Biscuits and [[blue sauce]]. 42-RST commented that if they were caught, they could claim that the food was consumables. In the droid's opinion, it would have been a reasonable explanation given the amount Bung ate. Leaving Sullust, the ''Chubby Gundark'' was intercepted by an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] commanded by Captain [[Wankle]]. The transport was taken aboard the Imperial [[Unidentified Imperial-class Star Destroyer (Sullust)|vessel]] and inspected, where a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] discovered the crates of Bantha Breakfast Biscuits. Learning that there was also an ample supply of blue sauce, Wankle ordered the confiscation of all but one crate and announced that there would be a special meal for the crew that night. Wankle allowed the ''Gundark'' to go on its way, although Bung was furious at the Imperial captain's actions and upset over the loss of the cargo. Bung piloted the ''Gundark'' to the planet [[Gastrula]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], where 42-RST negotiated with the [[Gastrulan]]s to buy the remaining crate of Breakfast Biscuits. The sale created a market for the food item, and the [[Great Bantha Breakfast Biscuit Run]] between Sullust and the Gastrula was established. Over the course of two [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]], Bung and 42-RST cleared several hundred thousand [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] shipping the Biscuits to the native Gastrulans. Unfortunately for Bung, he did not know that Bantha Breakfast Biscuits were subject to a strange anomaly that caused them to mutate into a lifeform known as the [[Giant Amorphous Bantha Breakfast Biscuit]]. The preservatives in the Biscuit combined with the stale air in the ''Gundark''{{'s}} hold and the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] on Gastrula, causing the Biscuits to mutate, unleashing the amorphous lifeform. The Giant Amorphous Bantha Breakfast Biscuits set out to consume all life on Gastrula. [[File:GiantAmorphBantha.jpg|thumb|left|A Giant Amorphous Bantha Breakfast Biscuit]] Unaware of this turn of events, Bung was returning to Sullust with two crates of the Biscuits when the contents of one crate mutated and broke free. The Giant Amorphous Bantha Breakfast Biscuit began to eat anything it could, including the [[Cargo barge (Nyubba-class)|barges]] being hauled by the ''Chubby Gundark''. Bung was forced to jettison a barge after it developed a [[Hull/Legends|hull]] breach, bemoaning the fact that it was company property and he would have to pay for it. Determined to save the ship, Bung took his [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and ventured into the barges to deal with the creature. The lifeform startled the Sullustan as he was searching for it, coming up behind him. After a brief chase, Bung eventually came to a dead end and was cornered by the Giant Amorphous Bantha Breakfast Biscuit. Sure that he was next on the creature's menu, he was surprised when the creature became frightened and run away. Bung searched for the answer and found that he was surrounded by boxes of blue sauce. Since the condiment was the recommended accompaniment to a Bantha Breakfast Biscuit, the captain reasoned that it was also the creature's weakness. Bung took a tube of the sauce, confronted the Giant Amorphous Bantha Breakfast Biscuit, and squeezed the contents over it. He was relieved when the creature [[Death/Legends|exploded]]. Returning to the cockpit of the ''Gundark'', Bung had to pick up 42-RST, who had ejected in an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] in an effort to save herself. Bung set 42-RST the task of cleaning up the mess left by the remains of the Giant Amorphous Bantha Breakfast Biscuit and noted that they would have to get the ship cleaned professionally and replace the lost cargo and barges. While they had made a lot of money from running Bantha Breakfast Biscuits, they would be lucky to break even with the extra expenses. 42-RST asked Bung about the last remaining crate of Biscuits and what they should do with it. Bung had something special planned for the crate and allowed the ship to be boarded by Captain Wankle on their return to Sullust. Without argument, Bung handed over the crate, but apologized to Wankle that he had no blue sauce to go with it. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Don't you think of anything but food?''"
"''You only say that because you're a droid.|42-RST and Bungo Bung|}} [[File:BungoBung_BW.jpg|thumb|right|Bungo discovers the Giant Amorphous Bantha Breakfast Biscuit's weakness—blue sauce]] Bungo Bung was a trusting and gentle Sullustan. He was a daydreamer and longed to travel amongst the [[Star/Legends|stars]] as a youth. He also had a voracious appetite and was noted for his love of Bantha Breakfast Biscuits. The relationship between him and his droid co-pilot 42-RST was one of mild antagonism, as the droid usually directed sarcastic comments at him. A loyal employee of SoroSuub Corporation, he did not like doing anything that would contravene the company's standing regulations, including using the ''Chubby Gundark'' to haul non-approved products, or jettisoning company property even when the ship was in danger of destruction. He was unaware of the true relationship between the Empire and SoroSuub Corporation—the Sullustan company had an agreement in place with the Empire to protect its holdings and to ensure it prospered under the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]]. The arrangement had even led the Corporation to take control of the Sullust planetary government. If Bung had known of this, he would have been at the forefront of any opposition. However, this would have been mostly to calm any ulcer he may have developed from worrying about it. ==Skills and abilities== Bungo Bung could use a blaster, and was knowledgeable about [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]] and [[Star system/Legends|planetary systems]]. A qualified pilot, Bung could fly and repair space transports, and had experience using the weaponry aboard starships. He could also [[Astrogation/Legends|plot courses]] through [[Realspace/Legends|space]] and hyperspace. ==Equipment== Bungo Bung dressed in black [[Spacer/Legends|spacer's]] garb, with a multi-pocketed sleeveless vest worn over the top of it. He carried a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]] on his person along with a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] and [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]]. He operated the ''Chubby Gundark'', a ''Nyubba''-class cargo barge driver, on behalf of the SoroSuub Corporation for whom he worked, and was the legal registered owner of 42-RST, his droid co-pilot. 42-RST came into his possession after Bung worked for the Mephout; they presented the droid as a gift to the Sullustan. While Bung considered it to be a reward for services rendered, the Mephout were merely phasing out obsolete technology. ==Behind the scenes== Bungo Bung was created by [[James Anderson]] for the story ''[[Bungo n' Rusti Get Carry-Out]]'' that appeared in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 11|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 11]], published by [[West End Games]] in [[November]] [[1996]].[[Star Wars Adventure Journal 11|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 11]] The story was both written and illustrated by Anderson. Originally, Anderson had planned to write a series of comic adventures starring Bungo and Rusti, but since [[Dark Horse Comics]] was the sole comic licensee the idea had to be abandoned. Dark Horse overlooked the publication of ''Bungo n' Rusti Get Carry-Out'' since they believed it to be a one-shot story.{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/comics/fancomics/bungo&rusti/faqs.html|text=Bungo n' Rusti Frequently Asked Questions|author=[[James Anderson]]|work=[[TheForce.net]]|archivedate=20160101200202}} However, he has made many appearances in the unlicensed ''[[Bungo n' Rusti]]'' stories by Anderson that can be found on [[TheForce.net]], which include ''Bungo n' Rusti Get the Record!'', ''[[101 Battle Neks]]'', ''Bungo n' Rusti On Strike!'', ''Castaways'', ''Bungo n' Rusti Join the Rebellion?!! Part I'', and ''Bungo n' Rusti Join the Rebellion?!! Part II''.{{WebCite|url=https://www.theforce.net/comics/fancomics/bungo&rusti/index.shtml|text=Bungo and Rusti Star Wars Comics!|author=[[James Anderson]]|work=[[TheForce.net]]|archivedate=20210725123043}} ==Appearances== *{{JournalCite|11|Bungo n' Rusti Get Carry-Out}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{CollectorCite|2|ComicScan|ComicScan: Rare, but Sometimes Well-Done}} *{{CollectorCite|4|ComicScan|ComicScan: Comics That Almost Were}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Bungo Bung}} {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/comics/fancomics/bungo&rusti/bungo.html|text=Bungo Bung|author=[[James Anderson]]|work=[[TheForce.net]]|archivedate=20160103074945}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Bung, Bungo}} [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Pilots]] [[Category:Sullustans]]
